# Welcome to PyNMR-STAR!

A Python module for reading, writing, and manipulating NMR-STAR files.

{{ License }} {{ Wheel }} {{ PythonVersions }}

## Overview

This library was developed by the BMRB to give the Python-using NMR
community tools to work with the NMR-STAR data format. It is used
internally and is actively maintained. The library is thoroughly
documented such that calling `help(object_or_method)` from an
interactive python session will print the documentation for the object
or method. An even more convenient way to learn how to use this library
is to read the documentation at [read the docs](https://pynmrstar.readthedocs.org).

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

modules.name: pynmrstar.remove_non_nef_data
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

modules.name: pynmrstar.utils
documentation: This file contains various helper functions.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.diff
documentation:
Prints the differences between two entries. Non-equal entries
will always be detected, but specific differences detected depends
on the order of entries.

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.format_category
documentation:
Adds a '_' to the front of a tag (if not present) and strips out
anything after a '.'

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.format_tag
documentation: Strips anything before the '.'

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.format_tag_lc
documentation: Strips anything before the '.' and makes the tag lowercase.

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.get_schema
documentation:
If passed a schema (not None) it returns it. If passed none,
it checks if the default schema has been initialized. If not
initialized, it initializes it. Then it returns the default schema.
noinspection PyDefaultArgument

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.iter_entries
documentation:
Returns a generator that will yield an Entry object for every
macromolecule entry in the current BMRB database. Perfect for performing
an operation across the entire BMRB database. Set `metabolomics=True`
in order to get all the entries in the metabolomics database.

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.quote_value
documentation:
Automatically quotes the value in the appropriate way. Don't
quote values you send to this method or they will show up in
another set of quotes as part of the actual data. E.g.:

quote_value('"e. coli"') returns ''"e. coli"''

while

quote_value("e. coli") returns "'e. coli'"

This will automatically be called on all values when you use a str()
method (so don't call it before inserting values into tags or loops).

Be mindful of the value of STR_CONVERSION_DICT as it will effect the
way the value is converted to a string.

functions.name: pynmrstar.utils.validate
documentation: Prints a validation report of an object.

modules.name: pynmrstar.definitions
documentation:
NMR-STAR definitions and other module parameters live here. Technically
you can edit them, but you should really know what you're doing.

Adding key->value pairs to STR_CONVERSION_DICT will automatically convert tags
whose value matches "key" to the string "value" when printing. This allows you
to set the default conversion value for Booleans or other objects.

WARNINGS:
 * STR_CONVERSION_DICT cannot contain both booleans and arithmetic types.
   Attempting to use both will cause an issue since boolean True == 1 in python
   and False == 0.

 * You must call utils.quote_value.clear_cache() after changing the
   STR_CONVERSION_DICT or else your changes won't take effect due to caching!

   The only exception is if you set STR_CONVERSION_DICT before performing any
   actions which would call quote_value() - which include calling __str__ or
   format() on Entry, Saveframe, and Loop objects.
!/usr/bin/python3

modules.name: pynmrstar.exceptions
documentation: Exceptions defined by PyNMR-STAR.

classes.name: pynmrstar.exceptions.ParsingError
documentation:
Indicates that something went wrong when parsing NMR-STAR data.
A line number on which the exception occurred will be provided if
possible.

classes.name: pynmrstar.exceptions.InvalidStateError
documentation:
Indicates that the data as exists in the PyNMRSTAR objects is not
consistent with the NMR-STAR format or dictionary. This often means that
internal PyNMRSTAR attributes were modified directly rather than using the
appropriate getters/setters, since the library attempts to prevent actions
which would lead to such states.

functions.name: pynmrstar._internal._get_comments
documentation:
Loads the comments that should be placed in written files.

The default argument is mutable on purpose, as it is used as a cache for memoization.
noinspection PyDefaultArgument

functions.name: pynmrstar._internal._json_serialize
documentation: JSON serializer for objects not serializable by default json code

functions.name: pynmrstar._internal._get_url_reliably
documentation:
Attempts to load data from a URL, retrying the specified number of times with an exponential
backoff if rate limited. Fails immediately on 4xx errors that are not 403.

functions.name: pynmrstar._internal._get_entry_from_database
documentation:
Fetches an entry from the API (or falls back to the FTP site) in
as reliable and robust a way as possible. Used by Entry.from_database().

functions.name: pynmrstar._internal._interpret_file
documentation:
Helper method returns some sort of object with a read() method.
the_file could be a URL, a file location, a file object, or a
gzipped version of any of the above.

functions.name: pynmrstar._internal.get_clean_tag_list
pynmrstar._internal.write_to_file
documentation: Writes the object to the specified file in NMR-STAR format.

modules.name: pynmrstar
documentation:
This module provides {py:class}`pynmrstar.Entry`, {py:class}`pynmrstar.Saveframe`,

: {py:class}`pynmrstar.Loop`, and {py:class}`pynmrstar.Schema` objects.

It also provides some utility functions in {py:obj}`pynmrstar.utils`

Use python's built in help function for documentation.
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a BMRB schema. With no arguments the most
up-to-date schema will be fetched from the BMRB FTP site.
Otherwise pass a URL or a file to load a schema from using the
schema_file keyword argument.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.__repr__
documentation: Return how we can be initialized.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.__str__
documentation: Print the schema that we are adhering to.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.add_tag
documentation:
Adds the specified tag to the tag dictionary. You must provide:

1) The full tag as such:
    "_Entry_interview.Sf_category"
2) The tag type which is one of the following:
    "INTEGER"
    "FLOAT"
    "CHAR(len)"
    "VARCHAR(len)"
    "TEXT"
    "DATETIME year to day"
3) A python True/False that indicates whether null values are allowed.
4) The sf_category of the parent saveframe.
5) A True/False value which indicates if this tag is a loop tag.
6) Optional: The tag to order this tag behind when normalizing
   saveframes.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.convert_tag
documentation:
Converts the provided tag from string to the appropriate
type as specified in this schema.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.string_representation
documentation:
Prints all the tags in the schema if search is not specified
and prints the tags that contain the search string if it is.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.val_type
documentation:
Validates that a tag matches the type it should have
according to this schema.

methods.name: pynmrstar.schema.Schema.tag_key
documentation: Helper function to figure out how to sort the tags.

A BMRB schema. Used to validate NMR-STAR files. Unless you need to
choose a specific schema version, PyNMR-STAR will automatically load
a schema for you. If you do need a specific schema version, you can
create an object of this class and then pass it to the methods
which allow the specification of a schema.

classes.name: pynmrstar.parser.Parser

methods.name: pynmrstar.parser.Parser.get_token
documentation: Returns the next token in the parsing process.

methods.name: pynmrstar.parser.Parser.load_data
documentation:
Loads data in preparation of parsing and cleans up newlines
and massages the data to make parsing work properly when multi-line
values aren't as expected. Useful for manually getting tokens from
the parser.

methods.name: pynmrstar.parser.Parser.parse
documentation:
Parses the string provided as data as an NMR-STAR entry
        and returns the parsed entry. Raises ParsingError on exceptions.

        Set raise_parse_warnings to raise an exception if the file has
        something technically incorrect, but still parsable.

        Following is a list of the types of errors that would trigger
        raise_parse_warnings:

        * A loop with no data was found.
        * A loop with no tags or values was found.
        * A tag with an improper multi-line value was found.
        Multi-line values should look like this:
        
;
The multi-line
value here.
;

        but the tag looked like this:
        
; The multi-line
value here.
;
Parses an entry. You should not ever use this class directly.

classes.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__contains__
documentation: Check if the given item is present in the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__delitem__
documentation: Remove the indicated saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__eq__
documentation:
Returns True if this entry is equal to another entry, false
if it is not equal.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__getitem__
documentation: Get the indicated saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__init__
documentation:
You should not directly instantiate an Entry using this method.
Instead use the class methods:

{py:meth}`Entry.from_database`, {py:meth}`Entry.from_file`,
{py:meth}`Entry.from_string`, {py:meth}`Entry.from_scratch`,
{py:meth}`Entry.from_json`, and {py:meth}`Entry.from_template`

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__iter__
documentation: Yields each of the saveframes contained within the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__len__
documentation: Returns the number of saveframes in the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__repr__
documentation: Returns a description of the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__setitem__
documentation: Set the indicated saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.__str__
documentation: Returns the entire entry in STAR format as a string.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.category_list
documentation: Returns a list of the unique categories present in the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.empty
documentation: Check if the entry has no data. Ignore the structural tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.entry_id
documentation:
When read, fetches the entry ID.

When set, updates the entry ID for the Entry, and updates all the tags which
are foreign keys of the Entry_ID. (For example, Entry.ID and
Citation.Entry_ID will be updated, if present.)

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.frame_dict
documentation: Returns a dictionary of saveframe name -> saveframe object mappings.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.from_database
documentation:
Create an entry corresponding to the most up to date entry on
the public BMRB server. (Requires ability to initiate outbound
HTTP connections.)

Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.from_file
documentation:
Create an entry by loading in a file. If the_file starts with
http://, https://, or ftp:// then we will use those protocols to
attempt to open the file.

Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

Setting raise_parse_warnings to True will result in the raising of a
ParsingError rather than logging a warning when non-valid (but
ignorable) issues are found.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.from_json
documentation: Create an entry from JSON (serialized or unserialized JSON).

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.from_string
documentation:
Create an entry by parsing a string.


Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

Setting raise_parse_warnings to True will result in the raising of a
ParsingError rather than logging a warning when non-valid (but
ignorable) issues are found.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.from_scratch
documentation:
Create an empty entry that you can programmatically add to.
You must pass a value corresponding to the Entry ID.
(The unique identifier "xxx" from "data_xxx".)

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.from_template
documentation:
Create an entry that has all of the saveframes and loops from the
schema present. No values will be assigned. Specify the entry
ID when calling this method.

The optional argument 'all_tags' forces all tags to be included
rather than just the mandatory tags.

The optional argument 'default_values' will insert the default
values from the schema.

The optional argument 'schema' allows providing a custom schema.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.add_saveframe
documentation: Add a saveframe to the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.compare
documentation:
Returns the differences between two entries as a list.
Non-equal entries will always be detected, but specific differences
detected depends on order of entries.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.add_missing_tags
documentation:
Automatically adds any missing tags (according to the schema)
to all saveframes and loops and sorts the tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.delete_empty_saveframes
documentation: Deprecated. Please use `py:meth:pynmrstar.Entry.remove_empty_saveframes`.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.format
documentation:
The same as calling str(Entry), except that you can pass options
to customize how the entry is printed.

skip_empty_loops will omit printing loops with no tags at all. (A loop with null tags is not "empty".)
skip_empty_tags will omit tags in the saveframes and loops which have no non-null values.
show_comments will show the standard comments before a saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_json
documentation:
Returns the entry in JSON format. If serialize is set to
False a dictionary representation of the entry that is
serializeable is returned instead.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_loops_by_category
documentation: Allows fetching loops by category.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_saveframe_by_name
documentation: Allows fetching a saveframe by name.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_saveframes_by_category
documentation: Allows fetching saveframes by category.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_saveframes_by_tag_and_value
documentation: Allows fetching saveframe(s) by tag and tag value.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_tag
documentation:
Given a tag (E.g. _Assigned_chem_shift_list.Data_file_name)
return a list of all values for that tag. Specify whole_tag=True
and the [tag_name, tag_value] pair will be returned.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.get_tags
documentation:
Given a list of tags, get all of the tags and return the
results in a dictionary.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.normalize
documentation:
Sorts saveframes, loops, and tags according to the schema
provided (or BMRB default if none provided).

Also re-assigns ID tag values and updates tag links to ID values.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.print_tree
documentation:
Prints a summary, tree style, of the frames and loops in
the entry.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.remove_empty_saveframes
documentation:
This method will remove all empty saveframes in an entry
(the loops in the saveframe must also be empty for the saveframe
to be deleted). "Empty" means no values in tags, not no tags present.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.remove_saveframe
documentation:
Removes one or more saveframes from the entry. You can remove saveframes either by passing the saveframe
object itself, the saveframe name (as a string), or a list or tuple of either.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.rename_saveframe
documentation:
Renames a saveframe and updates all pointers to that
saveframe in the entry with the new name.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.validate
documentation:
Validate an entry in a variety of ways. Returns a list of
errors found. 0-length list indicates no errors found. By
default all validation modes are enabled.

validate_schema - Determines if the entry is validated against
the NMR-STAR schema. You can pass your own custom schema if desired,
otherwise the cached schema will be used.

validate_star - Determines if the STAR syntax checks are ran.

methods.name: pynmrstar.entry.Entry.write_to_file
documentation:
Writes the entry to the specified file in NMR-STAR format.

Optionally specify:
show_comments=False to disable the comments that are by default inserted. Ignored when writing json.
skip_empty_loops=False to force printing loops with no tags at all (loops with null tags are still printed)
skip_empty_tags=True will omit tags in the saveframes and loops which have no non-null values.
format_=json to write to the file in JSON format.

An object oriented representation of a BMRB entry. You can initialize this
object several ways; (e.g. from a file, from the official database,
from scratch) see the class methods below.

classes.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__contains__
documentation: Check if the saveframe contains a tag or a loop name.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__delitem__
documentation: Remove the indicated tag or loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__eq__
documentation:
Returns True if this saveframe is equal to another saveframe,
False if it is equal.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__getitem__
documentation: Get the indicated loop or tag.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__iter__
documentation: Yields each of the loops contained within the saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__len__
documentation: Return the number of loops in this saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__lt__
documentation:
Returns True if this saveframe sorts lower than the compared
saveframe, false otherwise. The alphabetical ordering of the
saveframe category is used to perform the comparison.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__init__
documentation:
Don't use this directly. Use the class methods to construct:
{py:meth}`Saveframe.from_scratch`, {py:meth}`Saveframe.from_string`,
{py:meth}`Saveframe.from_template`, {py:meth}`Saveframe.from_file`,
{py:meth}`Saveframe.from_json`

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.category
documentation:
Updates the saveframe category. Sets the Sf_category tag if not present,
updates it if present.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.empty
documentation: Check if the saveframe has no data. Ignore the structural tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.loop_dict
documentation: Returns a hash of loop category -> loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.name
documentation: Returns the name of the saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.name
documentation: Updates the saveframe name.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.tag_dict
documentation: Returns a hash of (tag name).lower() -> tag value.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.from_scratch
documentation:
Create an empty saveframe that you can programmatically add
to. You may also pass the tag prefix as the second argument. If
you do not pass the tag prefix it will be set the first time you
add a tag.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.from_file
documentation:
Create a saveframe by loading in a file. Specify csv=True is
the file is a CSV file. If the_file starts with http://,
https://, or ftp:// then we will use those protocols to attempt
to open the file.

Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

Setting raise_parse_warnings to True will result in the raising of a
ParsingError rather than logging a warning when non-valid (but
ignorable) issues are found.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.from_json
documentation: Create a saveframe from JSON (serialized or unserialized JSON).

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.from_string
documentation:
Create a saveframe by parsing a string. Specify csv=True is
the string is in CSV format and not NMR-STAR format.

Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

Setting raise_parse_warnings to True will result in the raising of a
ParsingError rather than logging a warning when non-valid (but
ignorable) issues are found.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.from_template
documentation:
Create a saveframe that has all of the tags and loops from the
schema present. No values will be assigned. Specify the category
when calling this method. Optionally also provide the name of the
saveframe as the 'name' argument.

The optional argument 'all_tags' forces all tags to be included
rather than just the mandatory tags.

The optional argument 'default_values' will insert the default
values from the schema.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__repr__
documentation: Returns a description of the saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__setitem__
documentation: Set the indicated loop or tag.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.__str__
documentation:
Returns the saveframe in STAR format as a string. Please use {py:meth}`Saveframe.format`
when you want to pass arguments.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.add_loop
documentation: Add a loop to the saveframe loops.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.add_tag
documentation:
Add a tag to the tag list. Does a bit of validation and
parsing.

Set update to True to update a tag if it exists rather
than raise an exception.

Set convert_data_types to True to convert the tag value from str to
whatever type the tag is as defined in the schema.

Optionally specify a schema if you don't want to use the default schema.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.add_tags
documentation:
Adds multiple tags to the list. Input should be a list of
tuples that are either [key, value] or [key]. In the latter case
the value will be set to ".".  Set update to true to update a
tag if it exists rather than raise an exception.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.add_missing_tags
documentation:
Automatically adds any missing tags (according to the schema)
and sorts the tags.

Set recursive to False to only operate on the tags in this saveframe,
and not those in child loops.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.compare
documentation:
Returns the differences between two saveframes as a list.
Non-equal saveframes will always be detected, but specific
differences detected depends on order of saveframes.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.delete_tag
documentation: Deprecated, please see {py:meth}`pynmrstar.Saveframe.remove_tag`.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.get_data_as_csv
documentation:
Return the data contained in the loops, properly CSVd, as a
string. Set header to False omit the header. Set show_category
to False to omit the loop category from the headers.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.format
documentation:
The same as calling str(Saveframe), except that you can pass options
to customize how the saveframe is printed.

skip_empty_loops will omit printing loops with no tags at all. (A loop with null tags is not "empty".)
skip_empty_tags will omit tags in the saveframe and child loops which have no non-null values.
show_comments will show the standard comments before a saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.get_json
documentation:
Returns the saveframe in JSON format. If serialize is set to
False a dictionary representation of the saveframe that is
serializeable is returned.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.get_loop
documentation: Return a loop based on the loop name (category).

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.get_loop_by_category
documentation: Deprecated. Please use {py:meth}`pynmrstar.Saveframe.get_loop` instead.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.get_tag
documentation:
Allows fetching the value of a tag by tag name. Returns
a list of all matching tag values.

Specify whole_tag=True and the [tag_name, tag_value] pair will be
returned instead of just the value

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.loop_iterator
documentation: Returns an iterator for saveframe loops.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.print_tree
documentation: Prints a summary, tree style, of the loops in the saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.remove_loop
documentation:
Removes one or more loops from the saveframe. You can remove loops either by passing the loop object itself,
the loop category (as a string), or a list or tuple of either.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.remove_tag
documentation:
Removes one or more tags from the saveframe based on tag name(s).
Provide either a tag name or a list or tuple containing tag names.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.set_tag_prefix
documentation: Set the tag prefix for this saveframe.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.sort_tags
documentation:
Sort the tags so they are in the same order as a BMRB
schema. Will automatically use the standard schema if none
is provided.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.tag_iterator
documentation: Returns an iterator for saveframe tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.validate
documentation:
Validate a saveframe in a variety of ways. Returns a list of
errors found. 0-length list indicates no errors found. By
default all validation modes are enabled.

validate_schema - Determines if the entry is validated against
the NMR-STAR schema. You can pass your own custom schema if desired,
otherwise the schema will be fetched from the BMRB servers.

validate_star - Determines if the STAR syntax checks are ran.

methods.name: pynmrstar.saveframe.Saveframe.write_to_file
documentation:
Writes the saveframe to the specified file in NMR-STAR format.

Optionally specify:
show_comments=False to disable the comments that are by default inserted. Ignored when writing json.
skip_empty_loops=False to force printing loops with no tags at all (loops with null tags are still printed)
skip_empty_tags=True will omit tags in the saveframes and loops which have no non-null values.
format_=json to write to the file in JSON format.
A saveframe object. Create using the class methods, see below.

classes.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__contains__
documentation: Check if the loop contains one or more tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__eq__
documentation:
Returns True if this loop is equal to another loop, False if
it is different.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__getitem__
documentation: Get the indicated row from the data array.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__init__
documentation:
You should not directly instantiate a Loop using this method.
Instead use the class methods:

{py:meth}`Loop.from_scratch`, {py:meth}`Loop.from_string`,
{py:meth}`Loop.from_template`, {py:meth}`Loop.from_file`,
{py:meth}`Loop.from_json`

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__iter__
documentation: Yields each of the rows contained within the loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__len__
documentation: Return the number of rows of data.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__lt__
documentation:
Returns True if this loop sorts lower than the compared
loop, false otherwise.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__repr__
documentation: Returns a description of the loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__setitem__
documentation:
Set all of the instances of a tag to the provided value.
If there are 5 rows of data in the loop, you will need to
assign a list with 5 elements.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.__str__
documentation: Returns the loop in STAR format as a string.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.empty
documentation: Check if the loop has no data.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.from_file
documentation:
Create a loop by loading in a file. Specify csv=True if
the file is a CSV file. If the_file starts with http://,
https://, or ftp:// then we will use those protocols to attempt
to open the file.

Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

Setting raise_parse_warnings to True will result in the raising of a
ParsingError rather than logging a warning when non-valid (but
ignorable) issues are found.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.from_json
documentation: Create a loop from JSON (serialized or unserialized JSON).

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.from_scratch
documentation:
Create an empty saveframe that you can programmatically add
to. You may also pass the tag prefix as the second argument. If
you do not pass the tag prefix it will be set the first time you
add a tag.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.from_string
documentation:
Create a loop by parsing a string. Specify csv=True if
the string is in CSV format and not NMR-STAR format.

Setting convert_data_types to True will automatically convert
the data loaded from the file into the corresponding python type as
determined by loading the standard BMRB schema. This would mean that
all floats will be represented as decimal.Decimal objects, all integers
will be python int objects, strings and vars will remain strings, and
dates will become datetime.date objects. When printing str() is called
on all objects. Other that converting uppercase "E"s in scientific
notation floats to lowercase "e"s this should not cause any change in
the way re-printed NMR-STAR objects are displayed. Specify a custom
schema object to use using the schema parameter.

Setting raise_parse_warnings to True will result in the raising of a
ParsingError rather than logging a warning when non-valid (but
ignorable) issues are found.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.from_template
documentation:
Create a loop that has all of the tags from the schema present.
No values will be assigned. Specify the tag prefix of the loop.

The optional argument all_tags forces all tags to be included
rather than just the mandatory tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop._get_tags_from_schema
documentation:
Returns the tags from the schema for the category of this
loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop._check_tags_match_data
documentation:
Ensures that each row of the data has the same number of
elements as there are tags for the loop. This is necessary to
print or do some other operations on loops that count on the values
matching.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.add_data
documentation:
Add data to a loop. You can provide the data to add organized in four different ways, though the first
two are recommended for new code. The other two (#3 and #4) are preserved for sake of existing code (written
prior to version 3.3) and for niche use cases:

1: You can provide a list of dictionaries of tags to add. For example,
`[{'name': 'Jeff', 'location': 'Connecticut'}, {'name': 'Chad', 'location': 'Madison'}]` will add two new
rows, and set the values of the tags `name` and `location` to the values provided. If there are other
tags in the loop, they will be assigned null values for the rows corresponding to the tags added.

2: You can provide a dictionary of lists, as such (corresponds to adding the same ultimate data as in the
example #1): `{'name': ['Jeff', 'Chad'], 'location': ['Connecticut', 'Madison']}`. This will also create
two new rows in the loop and assign the values provided.

3: You can provide a list of lists of tag values to add. In this case, each list must have the same tag
values (and order of tags) as the known tags present in the loop. To correspond to the above examples, the data
would look like: `[['Jeff', 'Connecticut'], ['Chad', 'Madison']]`. Adding data this way requires both that
you provide values for all tags present in the loop, and that you provide the values in the same order that the
tags already are already defined in the loop.

4\. You can provide a single list of tag values to add. In the most simple case, that would correspond to just
adding one row of data in the same was as in #3 above, as such: `['Jeff', 'Connecticut']`. In a more
complicated example, you could also add data (corresponding to example #1 and #2) as such:
`['Jeff', 'Connecticut', 'Chad', 'Madison']` - but if you provide data this way, you must set

> `rearrange=True`. This usage is strongly discouraged, but exists for legacy reasons.

```
param data

See the docstring for the method.

type data

Union[List[dict], Dict[str, List], List[Union[str, float, int]], List[List[Any]]]

param convert_data_types

If true, converts data you provide into the data type defined in the dictionary.
For example, if you provided the string '5' for the tag _Atom_chem_shift.Val, it would automatically
be converted to a float while being added. This is mainly useful for parsers, as your data is probably
already in a format that is usable for you.

type convert_data_types

bool

param rearrange

If true, rearrange data provided in method #4 as necessary to fit in the loop. This only
exists for parsers, and it's use is strongly discouraged.

type rearrange

bool

param schema

A pynmrstar Schema object, which will be used to determine data types if convert_data_types
is True.

type schema

pynmrstar.Schema
```

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.add_data_by_tag
documentation:
Deprecated: It is recommended to use add_data() instead for most use
cases.

Add data to the loop one element at a time, based on tag.
Useful when adding data from SANS parsers.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.add_missing_tags
documentation:
Automatically adds any missing tags (according to the schema),
sorts the tags, and renumbers the tags by ordinal.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.add_tag
documentation:
Add a tag to the tag name list. Does a bit of validation
and parsing. Set ignore_duplicates to true to ignore attempts
to add the same tag more than once rather than raise an
exception.

You can also pass a list of tag names to add more than one
tag at a time.

Adding a tag will update the data array to match by adding
None values to the rows if you specify update_data=True.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.clear_data
documentation:
Erases all data in this loop. Does not erase the tag names
or loop category.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.compare
documentation:
Returns the differences between two loops as a list. Order of
loops being compared does not make a difference on the specific
errors detected.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.delete_tag
documentation: Deprecated. Please use `py:meth:pynmrstar.Loop.remove_tag` instead.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.delete_data_by_tag_value
documentation: Deprecated. Please use `py:meth:pynmrstar.Loop.remove_data_by_tag_value` instead.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.filter
documentation:
Returns a new loop containing only the specified tags.
Specify ignore_missing_tags=True to bypass missing tags rather
than raising an error.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.format
documentation:
The same as calling str(Loop), except that you can pass options
to customize how the loop is printed.

skip_empty_loops will omit printing loops with no tags at all. (A loop with null tags is not "empty".)
skip_empty_tags will omit tags in the loop which have no non-null values.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.get_data_as_csv
documentation:
Return the data contained in the loops, properly CSVd, as a
string. Set header to False to omit the header. Set
show_category to false to omit the loop category from the
headers.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.get_json
documentation:
Returns the loop in JSON format. If serialize is set to
False a dictionary representation of the loop that is
serializeable is returned.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.get_tag_names
documentation:
Return the tag names for this entry with the category
included. Throws ValueError if the category was never set.

To get the tags without the category, just access them directly
using the "tags" attribute.

To fetch tag values use get_tag().

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.get_tag
documentation:
Provided a tag name (or a list of tag names) return the selected tags by row as
a list of lists. Leave tags unset to fetch all tags.

If whole_tag=True return the full tag name along with the tag
value, or if dict_result=True, as the tag key.

If dict_result=True, return the tags as a list of dictionaries
in which the tag value points to the tag. Uses the specified capitalization
of the tag unless whole_tag is True, in which case it will use the capitalization
found in the loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.print_tree
documentation: Prints a summary, tree style, of the loop.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.remove_data_by_tag_value
documentation:
Removes all rows which contain the provided value in the
provided tag name. If index_tag is provided, that tag is
renumbered starting with 1. Returns the deleted rows.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.remove_tag
documentation:
Removes one or more tags from the loop based on tag name. Also removes any data for the given tag.
Provide either a tag or list of tags.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.renumber_rows
documentation:
Renumber a given tag incrementally. Set start_value to
initial value if 1 is not acceptable. Set maintain_ordering to
preserve sequence with offset.

E.g. 2,3,3,5 would become 1,2,2,4.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.set_category
documentation:
Set the category of the loop. Useful if you didn't know the
category at loop creation time.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.sort_tags
documentation:
Rearranges the tag names and data in the loop to match the order
from the schema. Uses the BMRB schema unless one is provided.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.sort_rows
documentation:
Sort the data in the rows by their values for a given tag
or tags. Specify the tags using their names or ordinals.
Accepts a list or an int/float. By default we will sort
numerically. If that fails we do a string sort. Supply a
function as key and we will order the elements based on the
keys it provides. See the help for sorted() for more details. If
you provide multiple tags to sort by, they are interpreted as
increasing order of sort priority.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.tag_index
documentation:
Helper method to do a case-insensitive check for the presence
of a given tag in this loop. Returns the index of the tag if found
and None if not found.

This is useful if you need to get the index of a certain tag to
iterate through the data and modify it.

methods.name: pynmrstar.loop.Loop.validate
documentation:
Validate a loop in a variety of ways. Returns a list of
errors found. 0-length list indicates no errors found. By
default all validation modes are enabled.

validate_schema - Determines if the entry is validated against
the NMR-STAR schema. You can pass your own custom schema if desired,
otherwise the schema will be fetched from the BMRB servers.

validate_star - Determines if the STAR syntax checks are ran.
A BMRB loop object. Create using the class methods, see below.
